arts.ttl: Conor Gallagher shows true colours with special Chelsea gesture after Atletico Madrid win
arts.sum: The 24-year-old left the club for Atletico Madrid in the summer transfer window for a fee worth £35.8million. After the 2-1 win, he was all smiles in a picture he shared with a number of youngsters from Chelsea's academy as they watched the game from the Wanda Metropolitano stands after travelling to Spain for the Madrid Football Cup.

arts.ttl: Julen Lopetegui under pressure but West Ham defiant amid sack concerns
arts.sum: Julen Lopetegui has lost his first three home league games of the season. The West Ham manager watched on from the London Stadium sidelines as Chelsea carved his Hammers wide open as the Blues cruised to a 3-0 win earlier on Saturday. Two first-half goals from Nicolas Jackson set the tone before Cole Palmer added […] The post Julen Lopetegui under pressure but West Ham defiant amid sack concerns appeared first on CaughtOffside.

arts.ttl: West Ham 0-3 Chelsea: Player ratings as impressive Blues cruise to away win
arts.sum: Palmer could have made it two minutes later, firing just wide of Areola's far post after more good work from Sancho, but Chelsea didn't have to wait long as gaping holes in West Ham's defence allowed Jackson to score a second. The impressive Mohammed Kudus had a goal ruled off for West Ham and Crysencio Summerville had a decent penalty appeal turned down by referee Sam Barrott, but that was as good as it got for the home side as Chelsea put the game to bed a minute after the half-time interval.

arts.ttl: I saw 'special' Chelsea player prove he could be one of the deals of the summer in West Ham win - opinion
arts.sum: He was solid at the London Stadium.

arts.ttl: Jackson stars as Chelsea beat West Ham to go second
arts.sum: Nicolas Jackson scored twice and produced an assist for Cole Palmer as Chelsea continued their impressive start to life under Enzo Maresca with a 3-0 win over local rivals West Ham United at the London Stadium. Jackson caught West Ham cold with the opener inside four minutes, slotting his finish through Alphonse Areola's legs after being played in by Jadon Sancho, who was making his first Chelsea start after assisting Christopher Nkunku's winner at AFC Bournemouth last Sunday.

arts.ttl: Chelsea dealt worst-case injury blow vs West Ham as Enzo Maresca reveals new Wesley Fofana plan
arts.sum: Enzo Maresca has downplayed suggestions that Wesley Fofana picked up an injury in Chelsea's 3-0 win over West Ham on Saturday. After three years of fitness problems for Fofana - who also pulled out of the France squad during the international break earlier this month - the sight of him with an ice pack and heavy strapping on his left knee had caused immediate concern after the match.

arts.ttl: The Mauricio Pochettino undroppable stopping Enzo Maresca fully unleashing Chelsea game-changer
arts.sum: Even his match-winning strike in last Saturday's late 1-0 win at Bournemouth wasn't enough to see him rewarded with a starting spot for Saturday's trip to West Ham. Instead, Nicolas Jackson was preferred to him up front and Jadon Sancho got the nod over him on the left flank . That has proved to be Nkunku's only Premier League start from the team's first five games, with the player instead having to settle for a starting role in their two-legged tie against Servette in the Europa Conference League play-offs.

arts.ttl: West Ham boss Julen Lopetegui calls his team 'soft' while skipper Jarrod Bowen says Hammers were 'very easy' to beat as Chelsea secure bragging rights in London derby
arts.sum: KATHRYN BATTE AT THE LONDON STADIUM: Julen Lopetegui branded his team as 'soft' while Jarrod Bowen admitted they made it easy for Chelsea after succumbing to a third straight home loss.

arts.ttl: 'Has not been contested': BBC Sport pundit claims West Ham didn't get anywhere near £32m Chelsea player
Chelsea owners ‘happy’ after victory over lacklustre West Ham
arts.sum: Todd Boehly and Behdad Eghbali both visited the dressing room after the 3-0 thumping.
